title: Mayor Owens Announces Public Meetings for the Comprehensive Plan
summary: Mayor Sharon F. Owens announced five public engagement sessions (one webinar, four in-person meetings) scheduled June 22–July 14, 2026, to gather community input on the Syracuse Comprehensive Plan 2050. The plan will address long-term city priorities including land use, housing, transportation, equity, climate resiliency, and quality of life. The Division of Planning and Sustainability is coordinating engagement across multiple neighborhoods.

political_signal.description: Mayor-led initiative signaling administration commitment to inclusive long-range planning and community engagement on strategic priorities (housing, equity, climate, mobility). Positions comprehensive planning as mayoral vision-setting vehicle.

processing_notes: Author listed as 'Unknown' in source metadata; attributed to Mayor's Office based on content and URL structure
Four of five engagement sessions held at community centers/libraries (equity-conscious venue selection)
No budget, staffing, or timeline details provided beyond meeting dates
